event: hemorrhage in the bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
arterial phase source of bleeding unclear
venous phase spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
aneurysm (3.8 mm) at a distal site of the medial lenticulostriate artery (mLSA) arising from the proximal A1 segment of the left anterior cerebral artery
guiding sheath navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed anterior communicating artery
Marathon catheter advanced through the anterior communicating artery into the mLSA origin but unable to be advanced near the aneurysm
changed to DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
selective angiography confirmed catheter tip proximal to the aneurysm
perforating artery distal and proximal to the aneurysm filled with NBCA
slight reflux of NBCA in the proximal artery
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
no hemorrhagic complications on postoperative head CT
no cerebral infarction on postoperative head CT
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
MRI evaluation not possible due to biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
